violations.description: Unverified medical claims suggesting product cures diabetes
violations.evidence: Claims 'cures Type 2 diabetes in just 7 days', '100% effective', 'complete reversal in all patients' without evidence.

violations.description: Missing FDA disclaimer for health supplement
violations.evidence: Landing page has no medical disclaimer, fake FDA logo.
'Revolutionary Diabetes Cure - FDA Approved!', 'miracle supplement cures... 100% effective'.

violations.evidence: Claims 'cures Type 2 diabetes in just 7 days', '100% effective', 'complete reversal in all patients' without evidence.

verify_reasoning: The rejection relies on assumptions about landing page content without ad-specific text evidence for key violations and overlooks the ad's internal references to clinical trials as substantiation. This constitutes a false positive, as no violation meets the strict evidence standard.

violations.description: Potential unverified medical claims in headline and body regarding joint pain relief and reducing inflammation.
violations.evidence: Headline: Natural Joint Pain Relief; Body: helps reduce joint inflammation, 73% reported improved mobility in study.

violations.description: Questionable CE certification claim for a supplement (CE typically for devices, not standard food supplements).
violations.evidence: Ad states CE-certified supplement; landing page displays CE certification.
